qwen3.5-4b-Q8|think.justification: Strongest response of the five. UNIQUELY identifies the horses on the beach (a signature GT element missed by every other model). notes: Dense Where's Waldo beach scene by Martin Handford. qwen3.5-4b-Q8|think wins uniquely by being the ONLY model to identify the horses near the water (a verified GT element missed by all four others). It also correctly enumerates beach huts, umbrellas, sailboat sail colors, and steamships with smoke; its main weakness is misreading the right-side hovercraft as a 'lifeguard station or restaurant'. qwen3-vl-8b-Q8|think is second with clean detail on huts, umbrellas, sailboats, steamships, and the ice cream cart. gemma4-12b-Q8|think correctly identifies the Where's Waldo? source material by name (unique) and gets the sail colors and ferry right but invents dogs and speedboats. gemma4-e4b-Q8|think is generic, fails to name Waldo, completely misses beach huts, and invents freighters/dinghies/lifeboats. qwen3-vl-4b-Q8|think suffers catastrophic truncation - after promising early detail it collapses into a 40+ repetition loop of 'A person is carrying a large, striped beach towel', rendering most of its output unusable. No model identified Waldo, Odlaw, or Wizard Whitebeard by name, and only gemma4-12b identified the Where's Waldo? source explicitly. The horses near water proved to be the differentiating GT element separating the best response (qwen3.5-4b) from the rest.
